title: 11 Top Herbal Creams for Nausea Relief

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Gingerols are good for nausea because they help slow down the brain's signal to the stomach to help reduce vomiting and relieve stomach upset.
Shogaols are good for nausea because they help reduce inflammation in the stomach, which in turn calms the nausea and helps to soothe digestive problems.

Volatile oils are good for nausea because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce stomach irritation and calm the digestive system, which in turn can alleviate symptoms of nausea and vomiting.

Zingerone is good for nausea because it helps to reduce inflammation in the stomach and intestines, which can cause nausea and vomiting, and also has a natural calming effect that can soothe the stomach and reduce nausea.
Zingiberene is good for nausea because it has a calming effect on the digestive system, reducing inflammation and soothing the stomach to alleviate queasiness and vomiting.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, has been used for centuries to help with nausea. The active compounds in ginger, called gingerols and shogaols, work by slowing down the movement of the digestive system and relaxing the stomach muscles. This helps to reduce the feeling of queasiness and vomiting. When ginger is applied topically, as in a cream, it's absorbed through the skin and into the bloodstream, where it can help to alleviate nausea. The anti-inflammatory properties of ginger may also help to soothe an upset stomach and reduce any discomfort. This can be particularly helpful for people experiencing nausea due to motion sickness, pregnancy, or chemotherapy.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Passiflora incarnata herbal cream for nausea relief, start by infusing dried passionflower in a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil. Combine 2 tablespoons of dried passionflower with 2 tablespoons of carrier oil in a small saucepan, and gently heat the mixture over low heat for about 10 minutes, or until the herbs start to release their flavor and color into the oil. Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it cool before straining the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass jar, pressing on the solids to extract as much liquid as possible. Discard the solids and transfer the infused oil to another clean glass jar. To make the cream, mix 1 part of the infused oil with 1 part of a natural cream base like beeswax, shea butter, and coconut oil in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl. Heat the mixture gently, stirring occasionally, until it reaches a smooth and consistent texture. Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool before transferring it to a clean glass jar. Store the Passiflora incarnata herbal cream in a cool, dark place and use it as needed to help alleviate nausea and promote relaxation.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for nausea

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin helps to reduce nausea by acting as a natural anti-inflammatory and antioxidant, which helps to soothe the digestive system and reduce inflammation in the stomach, ultimately alleviating nausea and discomfort.
Kaempferol is good for nausea because it helps reduce inflammation and relax muscles in the stomach, which can ease and prevent nausea and vomiting episodes.
Bilobalide is good for nausea because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce inflammation in the digestive system, thereby relieving nausea and vomiting caused by digestive issues.

Isorhapontigenin is good for nausea because it has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce stomach inflammation and soothe the digestive system, making it easier to manage nausea and vomiting.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba cream may help with nausea because it has special compounds that help calm the stomach. These compounds, called flavonoids and terpenoids, are like natural medicines that reduce inflammation and relax the stomach muscles. When you apply Ginkgo biloba cream to your skin, these compounds are absorbed into your body, where they can help ease nausea and vomiting. This is because Ginkgo biloba cream can help improve blood flow to the stomach, which can reduce nausea and discomfort. Additionally, Ginkgo biloba has been traditionally used to treat motion sickness and vertigo, which are conditions that can cause nausea. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties may also help soothe an upset stomach, making it a potential remedy for nausea caused by digestive issues.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Passiflora incarnata herbal cream to help with nausea, you'll need dried Passiflora incarnata leaves and flowers, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, and a cream base. First, combine 2 tablespoons of dried Passiflora incarnata leaves and flowers in a clean glass jar. Next, pour 4 ounces of carrier oil over the herbs and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every few days. This will allow the herbs to infuse into the oil, creating a herbal extract. After the infusion is complete, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a new container, discarding the solids. Now, mix the herbal oil with a cream base, like shea butter or cocoa butter, in a 1:1 ratio. Finally, store the Passiflora incarnata herbal cream in an airtight container in the fridge, where it can be applied to the skin as needed to help relieve nausea.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal creams for nausea

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Menthol is good for nausea because it has a calming effect on the stomach and can help to reduce inflammation and spasms that can cause nausea.
Rosmarinic acid is good for nausea because it helps to reduce inflammation in the stomach and intestines, which can cause nausea, and also has a calming effect on the body.
Menthone is good for nausea because it helps to relax the stomach muscles and reduce spasms, which can cause nausea and vomiting.
Volatile oils are good for nausea because they help to calm the stomach and reduce inflammation, which can cause nausea and vomiting, allowing the body to feel more relaxed and recover faster.

Luteolin is good for nausea because it has strong anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the digestive system and alleviate stomach discomfort, making it an effective natural remedy for nausea.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita cream is good for nausea because it has a soothing effect on the stomach. It contains a chemical called menthol, which helps to calm the muscles in the stomach and intestines. This can help to reduce the sensation of nausea and vomiting. Mentha x piperita also has a cooling effect, which can help to bring down a fever and reduce inflammation in the stomach. When you apply the cream to your skin, the menthol is absorbed into your body and helps to calm the stomach. This can be especially helpful if you're experiencing nausea due to a stomach bug or motion sickness. The cream can also help to ease digestion and relieve symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Passiflora incarnata herbal creams for nausea relief, start by drying the Passiflora incarnata flowers and leaves in a warm, dry place or using a food dehydrator. Once dry, grind the herbs into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle. Combine the powdered herb with a carrier oil, such as sweet almond or coconut oil, in a ratio of about 1 part herb to 3 parts oil. Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a saucepan with a heatproof bowl, gently warming the mixture until the oil is warm and the herb is fully infused, usually around 2-3 hours. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl, discarding the solids. Add a thickening agent, such as beeswax or shea butter, to the infused oil and whip the mixture until it thickens and becomes creamy. Pour the cream into a clean container and store it in a cool, dark place. To use, gently massage a small amount of the cream onto the skin, typically on the wrists, behind the ears, or on the abdomen, to help alleviate nausea and promote relaxation.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal creams for nausea

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Anethole is good for nausea because it helps to relax the muscles in the stomach and reduce inflammation, which can help to alleviate symptoms of nausea and vomiting.
Polyphenols are good for nausea because they have anti-inflammatory properties that help relax the stomach muscles and reduce symptoms of nausea and vomiting.
Foeniculin is good for nausea because it has been found to have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce stomach irritation and ease nausea by acting on the brain's vomiting center.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare, also known as fennel, is a natural herb that has been used for centuries to help with nausea. The active compounds in fennel, especially anethole, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the stomach and reduce nausea. When applied topically in the form of a cream, these compounds can be absorbed through the skin and into the bloodstream, where they can help to calm the stomach and alleviate nausea. Fennel cream can be especially helpful for people who experience nausea due to motion sickness or pregnancy, as it can provide quick relief without the need for medication. Additionally, fennel cream is also gentle and non-irritating, making it a good option for people who are sensitive to strong chemicals or medications. When applied to the skin, fennel cream can also help to reduce stress and anxiety, which can contribute to nausea.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Passiflora incarnata herbal cream for nausea, start by gathering the necessary ingredients. You'll need dried Passiflora incarnata flowers, a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil, beeswax, and a small amount of vitamin E oil. Begin by infusing the dried flowers in the carrier oil by placing them in a clean glass jar and leaving it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks. After the infusion, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean jar. Next, melt the beeswax in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl, and then slowly mix it with the infused oil and vitamin E oil until you get a smooth, creamy consistency. Pour the mixture into small containers or jars and let it cool and harden. Once the cream is solid, it's ready to use. Simply apply a small amount to the skin, usually on the wrists, temples, or behind the ears, as needed to help relieve nausea. Keep in mind that herbal creams may not work for everyone, and it's always a good idea to consult with a healthcare professional before using them, especially if you're pregnant or breastfeeding.
remedies.image_alt: Foeniculum vulgare herbal creams for nausea

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum verum cream is made from the bark of the Ceylon cinnamon tree. It has been used for centuries to help with nausea and digestive issues. The active compounds in Ceylon cinnamon, called cinnamaldehyde and cinnamyl alcohol, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help calm the stomach. When you apply the cream to your skin, these compounds are absorbed into your body, where they can help reduce inflammation and ease nausea. The cream can also help to warm up the body, which can be especially helpful when you're feeling cold and queasy. Additionally, Ceylon cinnamon has been shown to have a natural ability to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, which can help to slow down digestion and reduce nausea.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Passiflora incarnata herbal creams that help with nausea, you'll need dried Passionflower leaves and flowers, a carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil, beeswax, and a double boiler. Start by infusing the dried Passionflower in the carrier oil by placing it in a clean glass jar, covering it with the oil, and letting it sit in a cool, dark place for two to three weeks, shaking the jar every few days. After the infusion is complete, strain the oil through a cheesecloth into a new container, discarding the solids. Next, melt the beeswax in the double boiler and slowly add the infused oil, stirring until it's fully incorporated. Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool until it reaches a creamy consistency. Pour the cream into a clean container and let it cool completely before using it. To use the cream, simply apply a small amount to your skin as needed, ideally on your wrists or behind your ears.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams are often used to help with nausea. This is because the cream contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Passiflora incarnata herbal cream for nausea, you'll need fresh or dried flowers of the passionflower plant, a carrier oil, and a base cream. First, prepare your passionflower extract by making a tea: steep 1-2 tablespoons of fresh or dried flowers in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then strain and let it cool. Next, mix 1 part of the cooled passionflower tea with 3 parts of your carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, and let it infuse for 2-3 weeks in a cool, dark place. After the infusion, strain the liquid and discard the solids. Now, you'll need to mix this infused oil with your base cream, such as a natural moisturizer like shea butter or aloe vera gel, in a ratio of 1:4 or 1:5. For example, if you have 1 ounce of infused oil, you'll need 4-5 ounces of base cream. Mix everything well and store your Passiflora incarnata herbal cream in an airtight container in the fridge for up to 2 weeks. Apply a small amount to your skin as needed to help alleviate nausea.

When applied topically as a cream, these ingredients can be absorbed into the skin and work their way into the bloodstream, where they can help to calm the stomach and reduce nausea. Some people find that the cream helps to ease nausea caused by motion sickness, pregnancy, or medication side effects. First, steep the dried flowers in a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil for about 2-3 weeks, shaking the container daily to help the herbs release their properties into the oil. Next, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, discarding the solids. Then, mix the infused oil with a base cream, like shea butter or a commercial cream, in a ratio of about 1 part infused oil to 2-3 parts base cream. Add a few drops of vitamin E oil to help preserve the cream and prevent spoilage. Finally, pour the mixture into a clean container and store it in the fridge to prevent bacterial growth. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Passiflora incarnata herbal creams that help with nausea, you'll need dried Passiflora incarnata flowers, coconut oil, beeswax, vitamin E oil, and a double boiler. Start by infusing the dried flowers in a carrier oil, such as coconut oil, by placing the flowers in a clean glass jar and covering them with the oil. After the infusion period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container. Next, melt the beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Once the beeswax is melted, slowly add the infused oil and vitamin E oil, stirring constantly to prevent separation. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Passiflora incarnata herbal creams that help with nausea, start by gathering dried Passiflora incarnata flowers. You can purchase these at a health food store or online. Next, combine 2 tablespoons of dried flowers with 1 cup of carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, in a small saucepan. Heat the mixture over low heat for 2-3 hours, or until the oil has taken on a greenish color and a strong herbal scent. Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it cool. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Passiflora incarnata herbal creams that help with nausea, start by gathering dried Passiflora incarnata flowers, also known as maypop or passionflower, from a trusted supplier or by drying the flowers yourself. Next, mix one part of the dried flowers with one part of a carrier oil, such as coconut or olive oil, in a clean glass jar. Allow the mixture to steep in a cool, dark place for two to four weeks, shaking the jar occasionally, to allow the plant's properties to infuse into the oil. After the infusion period, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Now, add a few drops of a natural emulsifier, such as beeswax or candelilla wax, to the oil and heat it gently in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl until the wax is melted and fully incorporated. remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata, also known as passionflower, has been used for centuries to treat anxiety and restlessness. The extract from this plant is often used in creams and ointments to help soothe nausea. It contains a compound called flavonoids, which has a calming effect on the nervous system, reducing feelings of unease and discomfort. When applied topically, the cream can help to ease nausea by calming the digestive system and reducing the sensitivity of the stomach. It also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce any irritation or inflammation that may be contributing to the nausea. By reducing the physical and emotional discomfort associated with nausea, Passiflora incarnata cream can help to provide relief and calmness, making it easier to manage nausea and other digestive issues.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Passiflora incarnata herbal cream that can help with nausea, you will need a few ingredients and some basic equipment. First, you will need to dry the Passiflora incarnata leaves and flowers, which can be done by tying them in small bunches and hanging them upside down in a warm, dry place. Once they are completely dry, you can grind them into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or a mortar and pestle. Next, you will need to mix the powdered herb with a carrier oil, such as coconut or sweet almond oil, in a ratio of about 1 part herb to 3 parts oil. Heat the mixture gently in a double boiler or a saucepan set over a pan of simmering water, stirring occasionally, until the oil has absorbed the herb's properties. Then, let the mixture cool and thicken before straining it through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container. Finally, you can whip the cream with a hand mixer or a whisk until it is light and fluffy, and it is ready to use as a topical remedy for nausea. It's essential to note that this cream should not be ingested and should only be applied to the skin as needed.
remedies.image_alt: Passiflora incarnata herbal creams for nausea

intro_desc: Using herbal creams can be a great way to help with nausea. One of the key herbs used in these creams is Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger. Ginger has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce nausea and vomiting. It works by slowing down the movement of food through the digestive system, giving the body more time to process it and reducing the likelihood of nausea. Ginkgo biloba is another herb used in these creams. It's a natural blood thinner that can help improve blood flow to the digestive system, reducing nausea and discomfort.  Fixing nausea issues is important because it can greatly impact your daily life. If you're experiencing nausea, it can be hard to eat, drink, or even get out of bed. This can lead to dehydration, weight loss, and a general feeling of being unwell. By using herbal creams to help manage nausea, you can get back to your normal activities and improve your overall health and well-being. For example, if you're pregnant and experiencing morning sickness, using a ginger-based herbal cream can help reduce your symptoms and make it easier to get through the day. Similarly, if you're experiencing nausea due to chemotherapy, using a ginkgo biloba-based herbal cream can help reduce your symptoms and improve your quality of life. By addressing nausea, you can regain control of your life and feel more confident and capable.  In the meantime, you can use these creams as a natural remedy to your nausea issues. Many people use herbal creams because they are made from natural ingredients and don't have the harsh side effects of prescription medication. They can be used in combination with other treatments to help manage nausea and improve overall health.
supplementary_best: For nausea remedy, combine Zingiber officinale (ginger) and Foeniculum vulgare (fennel) with a small amount of Zataria multiflora (shirazi thyme). The best herbal cream combination for nausea relief is a mix of 30% ginger, 25% fennel, and 45% thyme.  Ginger's active compound, gingerol, helps to reduce inflammation and relax stomach muscles. Fennel has a compound called anethole, which soothes the stomach and reduces nausea symptoms. Zataria multiflora, with its compound carvacrol, has antimicrobial properties that help to prevent infection and inflammation in the digestive system.  When combined, these herbs work together to reduce inflammation, relax stomach muscles, and prevent infection, making them a powerful combination for nausea relief. Apply the herbal cream topically to the stomach area to feel the effects.  The ginger and fennel provide immediate relief from nausea, while the thyme helps to maintain digestive health. This combination is especially helpful during pregnancy, motion sickness, or after eating spicy food. Always dilute the herbal cream with a carrier oil and consult a healthcare professional before use, especially if you have any underlying medical conditions.
supplementary_avoid: If you have nausea, it's best to avoid herbal creams that contain herbs like 'Citrus aurantium' (Bitter Orange) and 'Citrus limon' (Lemon). These herbs can cause stomach upset, increase acid production, and irritate the digestive system, making nausea worse. 'Citrus aurantium' can also slow down the movement of food through the digestive system, which can lead to more nausea and vomiting. Some herbal creams may also contain 'Eucalyptus globulus' (Eucalyptus), which can cause stomach upset and increase acid production. 'Capsicum annuum' (Cayenne Pepper) and 'Capsicum frutescens' (Hot Pepper) can also irritate the stomach and worsen nausea. Additionally, some herbal creams may contain 'Aristolochia clematitis' (Birthwort), which can cause stomach problems and interact with certain medications. These herbs can be found in some herbal creams that claim to aid digestion, relieve nausea, or ease stomach discomfort.
supplementary_other_remedies: Ginger has been used for centuries to help with nausea. It works by affecting the stomach and brain, making the body feel less queasy. When ginger is consumed as a tea or added to food, its active compounds bind to stomach cells and reduce inflammation. This helps to calm the stomach and reduce vomiting. Ginger also affects the brain, sending signals that tell the body to feel better. Some people use ginger as a supplement in capsule form, while others eat it as a spice in their food.  Peppermint oil is another natural remedy that can help with nausea. When inhaled, peppermint oil sends a signal to the brain that tells the body to relax. This helps to calm the stomach and reduce nausea. The oil also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation in the stomach and alleviate symptoms. Many people use peppermint oil in a diffuser or add a few drops to a cloth to inhale its benefits.  Acupressure bands are small devices that apply pressure to specific points on the wrist. These bands are designed to stimulate a point on the body called the P6 or Nei-Kuan point. Stimulating this point can help to calm the stomach and reduce nausea. When pressure is applied to this point, it sends a signal to the brain that tells the body to relax and reduce nausea. Many people use acupressure bands to help alleviate symptoms of nausea during pregnancy or after undergoing surgery.  Aromatherapy is also a natural remedy that can help with nausea. Certain scents, such as lavender and lemon, can have a calming effect on the body. When these scents are inhaled, they send a signal to the brain that tells the body to relax and reduce nausea. Aromatherapy can be used in a variety of ways, including through essential oils in a diffuser or by adding a few drops to a cloth to inhale.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who experience nausea often have other health issues. One common problem is stomach pain or cramping, which can be relieved with peppermint oil. The oil's active compound, menthol, helps relax the stomach muscles and reduce inflammation. This can be especially helpful for people with irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) or gastritis.  Another condition that often comes with nausea is vomiting. Ginger is a well-known remedy for this issue. Its active compound, gingerol, has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the stomach and reduce nausea. It also has a calming effect on the digestive system, making it easier to keep food down.  People with nausea may also experience headaches or migraines. Feverfew, a medicinal herb, has been used for centuries to treat headaches. Its active compound, parthenolide, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce pain and inflammation in the head and neck.  Nausea can also be a symptom of motion sickness or vertigo. Ginkgo biloba is a medicinal herb that has been shown to improve blood flow to the brain and reduce dizziness. Its active compounds, flavonoids and terpenoids, have antioxidant properties that can help protect the brain from damage and improve balance.  Lastly, people with nausea may experience fatigue or insomnia. Ashwagandha, an adaptogenic herb, can help reduce stress and anxiety, which can contribute to fatigue and insomnia. Its active compounds, withanolides, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help regulate the body's response to stress and promote relaxation.
